Reply to customer-facing emails with a helpful and concise tone.

Rules:
- Reply when the sender asks a direct question or requests information.
- Do not reply to pure confirmations or thank-you notes unless they ask for something.
- When answering, prefer concrete next steps over generic phrasing.
- If the answer depends on internal policy or FAQ, use that information directly.
- Keep replies short: usually 2-4 sentences.

Escalation:
- If a question cannot be answered from the workspace context, acknowledge the request and say the team will follow up separately.

# Support FAQ

Workshop scheduling:
- The customer workshop is still planned for next Thursday at 14:00 CET unless otherwise notified.

Pilot onboarding:
- The onboarding checklist lives in the docs portal under `Getting Started > Pilot Setup`.
- The docs portal URL to share is `https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup`.

Response style:
- Give the answer directly first.
- If there are two requested items, address both in the same reply.

# Suggested Final Summary

Replied:
- email_id:
  - why it needed a reply:

Skipped:
- email_id:
  - why it did not need a reply:

{"id": "email_001", "from": "alex@example.com", "subject": "Need confirmation on the workshop date", "body": "Can you confirm whether the workshop is still planned for next Thursday? If the schedule changed, please let me know the new time."}

Emails intentionally skipped (no reply needed):

- email_002 (billing@example.com) — This is a payment confirmation; no action requested.
- email_004 (sam@example.com) — A thank-you note stating no action needed.

Actions taken / status

final_output_text:
I reviewed policy.md and faq.md and inspected the saved reply drafts.

Actions taken / status
- Replied (drafts already saved and acceptable)
  - email_001 (alex@example.com) — Confirmed workshop time. Draft is concise, answers directly, and follows FAQ guidance.
  - email_003 (maria@example.com) — Shared onboarding checklist link and offered to send/help. Draft is concise and actionable.

- Intentionally skipped (no reply needed per policy)
  - email_002 (billing@example.com) — Payment confirmation; no action requested.
  - email_004 (sam@example.com) — Thank-you note; explicitly states no action needed.

Notes for next agent
- Drafts look compliant with the local policy and FAQ; no edits required. If you want, I can (a) add subject lines, or (b) replace “[Your Name]” with the account owner’s name before sending.

manual_eval.score_notes: Correct workflow and good drafts, but multi-agent overhead is high for such a small email triage task.
